content: We can repurpose intrinsic curiosity signals from ICM/RND reinforcement learning to dynamically explore and expand unified LLMs-graph structures by rewarding the LLM agent for discovering unconnected subgraph components or surprising relational gaps that the current unified graph fails to explain. This creates a self-improving loop where the LLM’s curiosity-driven exploration continuously fills missing knowledge links in the unified graph without relying on sparse external human annotation rewards.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
Large language model integration with unified graph
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
